refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes a measurement unit ('measured in m³/h or ton/h') which is a solution detail related to how productivity is quantified rather than what the system must do. Removing measurement specifics keeps the FR solution-neutral. The FR contains a single obligation: supporting achievement of the productivity target, so it is atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through operational testing and data collection that the NGHE solution enables meeting or exceeding the customer-defined productivity target during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Monitor and report actual productivity metrics continuously
DP2: Adjust operational parameters dynamically to meet productivity targets
DP3: Provide feedback and alerts when productivity deviates from target
DP4: Integrate safety and operational policies into productivity management
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Monitor and report actual productivity metrics continuously

coupling_check.notes: Selecting continuous monitoring and reporting as the DP keeps the solution logically independent from control or adjustment mechanisms, supporting modularity and clear responsibility boundaries.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable productivity measurement methods
Risk that monitoring alone may not ensure achieving productivity targets without adjustment mechanisms
Assumes operational policies do not conflict with productivity measurement processes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing the FR further to separate measurement (monitoring) from control (adjustment) to maintain independence and clarity in DP selection.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for monitoring and reporting productivity metrics, integrating data collection, processing, and reporting functionalities.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from various sensors and subsystems and sends processed productivity metrics to external systems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends aggregated productivity reports to remote monitoring centers or cloud services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as productivity monitoring is a core function of the NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion since data collection and reporting are centralized here.
Partial containment possible as vehicle sensors provide raw data, but reporting and aggregation likely diffuse outside vehicle module.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on control rather than monitoring productivity metrics.
Very low containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety, not productivity monitoring.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing DP1 because it centralizes data collection, processing, and reporting of productivity metrics, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module for raw data acquisition may cause interface complexity.
If productivity metrics require real-time control feedback, some diffusion to Teleoperation may occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP1 directly addresses the FR by specifying continuous monitoring and reporting of actual productivity metrics, which supports achieving the customer-defined productivity target. The DP is logically aligned with the FR and grounded in measurable productivity data.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module for raw data acquisition may cause interface complexity.
If productivity metrics require real-time control feedback, some diffusion to Teleoperation may occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion, centralizing data collection, processing, and reporting. Containment scores support this, with NGHE Solution scoring highest in containment and lowest in diffusion. However, some diffusion risk exists due to dependencies on Vehicle sensors and possible real-time feedback needs involving Teleoperation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as productivity metrics can be measured and compared against the customer-defined productivity target during representative operations, per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The NGHE Solution module is clearly identified as the implementation boundary responsible for realizing DP1, with well-defined interfaces for data input (Telemetry) and output (Network Link). Ownership is unambiguous.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module to reduce complexity and potential diffusion.
Assess and document any real-time control feedback requirements that might involve Teleoperation to manage diffusion risk.
Consider defining submodules within NGHE Solution if complexity grows to maintain high cohesion and low diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating the current decomposition is sound and the implementation module is well defined with clear ownership and feasible verification. However, given the high safety criticality and noted diffusion risks related to interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, further decomposition of the FR into sub-FRs is warranted to manage complexity, clarify interfaces, and maintain high cohesion and low diffusion within the NGHE Solution module. This will also support addressing recommended revisions such as defining submodules and clarifying interface specifications.
next_level_sub_frs: Report productivity metrics to the network link
Interface with Vehicle module for raw data acquisition
Manage real-time control feedback involving Teleoperation

revise.why: To clarify interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module and to assess real-time control feedback requirements involving Teleoperation, potentially defining submodules within NGHE Solution to maintain high cohesion and low diffusion.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be further decomposed into submodules without violating architectural constraints.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can be clearly specified and managed.
Verification of sub-FRs remains feasible under high safety criticality.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity and diffusion with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Real-time control feedback requirements may increase diffusion risk.
Need to ensure submodule definitions do not introduce new diffusion or ownership ambiguities.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module.
Requirements and constraints for real-time control feedback involving Teleoperation.
Containment and diffusion metrics for proposed submodules within NGHE Solution.
